caseStudies.title: CRM Data Quality Overhaul
caseStudies.description: Cleaning and enriching a 2M-record customer database.
caseStudies.overview: A B2B enterprise had accumulated years of duplicated, incomplete, and outdated records in its CRM, undermining sales team confidence in the data and making marketing segmentation unreliable. The project aimed to establish a trusted, enriched customer database as the foundation for growth.
caseStudies.problem: An estimated 34% of CRM records contained duplicate entries, and 41% were missing critical firmographic data. Sales reps spent an average of 45 minutes per day manually verifying contact information before outreach.
caseStudies.solution: An automated data quality pipeline was built to deduplicate records using probabilistic matching, enrich missing fields via third-party data providers, and flag stale contacts for review. Ongoing data health monitoring was implemented with automated alerts when quality scores dropped below threshold.

results.summary: Sales teams now operate from a trusted data foundation, improving outreach effectiveness and pipeline predictability. Marketing campaigns benefit from reliable segmentation, improving ROI on demand generation spend.

caseStudies.title: Sales Process Automation
caseStudies.description: Eliminating manual handoffs across the sales cycle.
caseStudies.overview: A high-growth SaaS company's sales process was bottlenecked by manual handoffs between marketing, SDRs, account executives, and customer success teams. Deals slipped through the cracks during transitions, and leadership lacked real-time pipeline visibility.
caseStudies.problem: Lead routing was manual and inconsistent, with new inbound leads sometimes waiting over 24 hours for assignment. Contract generation required copying data between systems, introducing errors. CRM updates depended on rep discipline rather than automation.
caseStudies.solution: An automated lead routing engine was built with territory-based assignment and round-robin distribution. Contract generation was automated by pulling deal data directly from the CRM into pre-approved templates. CRM activities were auto-logged from email and calendar integrations, removing the manual update burden from reps.

results.summary: The automated sales process has become a key driver of the company's revenue growth. Leadership now has real-time pipeline visibility, enabling more accurate forecasting and faster course correction.

caseStudies.title: Customer 360 Integration
caseStudies.description: Unifying 8 data sources into a single customer view.
caseStudies.overview: A telecommunications provider's customer data was fragmented across billing, network, support, and marketing systems. Customer-facing teams had no unified view, resulting in poor experience during support calls and missed upsell opportunities.
caseStudies.problem: Support agents switched between 5 different systems to gather context before addressing a customer query, adding minutes to every call. Marketing campaigns were launched without awareness of active support tickets, damaging customer relationships further.
caseStudies.solution: A Customer 360 platform was built on top of the CRM, integrating data streams from all 8 source systems in real time. A unified customer timeline presented agents with the full history of billing events, support interactions, and product usage in a single interface. Marketing suppression lists were automatically updated based on open support cases.

results.summary: Support quality improved dramatically, and the unified view enabled contextually relevant upsell conversations that increased attach rates on premium products.
